Course Title: Using AI with Electronic Health Records (EHRs)
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare professionals, nurses, physicians, medical assistants, clinical staff, healthcare administrators, health information managers, medical records clerks, healthcare IT users
Team or Dept this is for: Clinical Operations, Health Information Management, Healthcare IT, Medical Records, Nursing, Quality Assurance, Compliance, Risk Management
Description: Risks of data exposure and incorrect summarisation.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with electronic health records systems and a general understanding of how AI tools are used in everyday workflows, though no advanced technical knowledge is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with data exposure when using AI tools alongside Electronic Health Records (EHRs), and explain how these risks can affect patient confidentiality and organisational compliance. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to recognise and reduce the impact of incorrect AI summarisation within clinical and healthcare settings.
